DISCOVERY DEPT. 302 AS TO FEB-14-2023 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO SITE INSPECTION REQUEST - DEFENDANT STIPULATES THE THIS MATTER HEARD BEFORE A JUDGE PRO TEM. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE MOTION IS DENIED AS IMPRPERLY NOTICED. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Site Inspection Request
Off Calendar Feb-14-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF CYNTHIA WARD's Motion To Compel Responses To Site Inspection Request

Appearance;
Paul Colllins, Esq. of TENANT LAW GROUP, PC, Tel: 888.510.7511, for plaintiff/moving party, via ZOOM videoconference;
No other appearance.

The tentative ruling was adopted on PLAINTIFF CYNTHIA WARD's Motion To Compel Responses To Site Inspection Request.

Transferred to be heard on the court's discovery calendar (room 301) on the same date at 9:00 a.m.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Proceedings not reported. (302/RBU)

PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO SITE INSPECTION REQUEST
Off Calendar Feb-14-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):

Atty. Farid Zakaria, Esq. ROPERS MAJESKI PC (Tele: 408-287-6262) for all Defendants
No appearance by Plaintiff

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO SITE INSPECTION REQUEST
Defendant stipulates to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge, plaintiff, and counsel for defendant authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling: The motion is denied as improperly noticed.

Defendant's counsel to prepare and submit a proposed order to opposing counsel for approval as to form, and then to the pro tem judge in Word format, to JPDeol@dhillonlaw.com.

Judge Pro Tem: John-Paul Deol; Clerk: R. Sahatjian; Not Reported =(302/JPT)
